meta.title: Ford Window Sticker Lookup: Check Used Ford's Original Build
meta.description: Get a Ford Window Sticker by VIN to confirm how your Ford left the factory. View the real build with accurate MSRP, trim, engine details, and equipment.

hero.heading: Find Your Ford’s Original Factory Window Sticker
hero.description: Not sure what your Ford really is? Pull the Ford window sticker by VIN and see the original factory build, including trim, equipment, packages, and other key specs. Handy for buyers and sellers.

whatIs.heading: What is a Ford Window Sticker?
paragraphs: A Ford window sticker is like the car’s factory receipt. It shows how the Ford was built when it was new, including the model and trim, the VIN, what came as standard, what was added as options, and the original price context (MSRP).

For European buyers and sellers, it’s a straightforward way to check what the car should have from day one. If the original sticker isn’t with the car anymore, Vehicle History Europe lets you retrieve a Ford Window Sticker by VIN as a downloadable digital copy you can keep or share during a sale.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a Ford Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: A Ford listing can be messy. A Ford window sticker puts the factory details in one place, so you can see what the Ford was built with from the start, including the identity info, equipment, options, key specs, and MSRP. Here’s what you’ll find once you pull a Ford window sticker by VIN.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: This is the plain-language identity of the car. Model year, Ford model name, derivative/series, plus the key basics that stop mix-ups. Ford uses different naming across markets, so this keeps you on the right version.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: Shows the official year and model family, so you’re not comparing a Focus to a Focus that’s actually a different generation.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: This is where trims like the Trend, Titanium, ST-Line, or Vignale, and body types (e.g., hatch, estate, SUV, van) show up. Knowing this helps you spot “trim upgrades” that are basically just badges.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: The VIN (or chassis number) is tied to the label, so you can cross-check it against the windscreen, door jamb, and documents.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: This section lists what Ford intended to come standard for that trim level. It’s not the fancy extras; it’s the baseline. This helps you compare trims quickly without opening ten tabs and losing your mind.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: This is the everyday stuff that shapes how the Ford feels: comfort features, convenience tech, and practical equipment listed for that build.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: On the window sticker, this part highlights safety and driver support items that should be present for that build. It lets you check what should be on the vehicle before you travel to see it.

categories.title: Optional Equipment
categories.description: This is the part that separates “basic spec” from “nice spec.” Factory-fitted options and upgrades tied to the VIN. This helps you avoid paying extra for options the car never had.

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: A single package can change the whole driving experience (e.g., lights, safety tech, heated bits, etc). The window sticker shows what bundle was fitted.

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: Single options added to the build, like upgraded seats, wheels, lighting, infotainment, or driver assist items (depending on the vehicle record). If you’re comparing two similar cars, this is the cleanest way to see what’s genuinely different.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: Some trims include extras that don’t show on lower trims, even if the seller calls them “options.” This section helps separate trim content from paid upgrades.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: This section gives you the original pricing context shown on the sticker. It’s not today’s used price. It’s the factory starting point. Giving you a reality anchor for what was “base” and what was paid as extras.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This was the manufacturer’s suggested base price from the sticker. Useful for comparing trims and understanding where the car originally sat in the range.

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: Two cars can look identical in photos. This breakdown shows what was added at the factory and helps explain why one was genuinely a higher-spec build.

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: The engine line is linked to the VIN, with build identifiers like aspiration, layout, displacement, and other factory details shown on the window sticker. This helps when you’re comparing running costs, insurance group expectations, or just trying to confirm what’s under the bonnet.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: Shows the engine identifiers listed for that Ford, such as displacement, cylinder layout, turbo/aspiration, and other details when listed. For example, the seller says “1.0 EcoBoost,” but the paperwork tells a slightly different story. This section helps you verify it.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: Shows whether the Ford was built with a manual or automatic gearbox, and often includes the exact transmission type listed for that VIN.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: Confirms how power is delivered (FWD/RWD/AWD). This is important information, because it can affect the winter usability, tyre setup expectations, and long-term driving feels

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: This performance block shows the key numbers in a simple format so you can compare trims properly. Even if you’re not racing anyone, it helps you avoid buying a “sporty badge” that doesn’t match the actual output.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: The window sticker listed the factory-rated power figures. This is great for comparing similar Ford trims and avoiding confusion between close variants

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: Shows torque and related performance lines from the sticker. The torque often explains why one Ford feels punchy, and another feels flat, even with similar horsepower numbers.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed
subItems.description: This section makes comparisons easier when you’re looking at two similar Fords, helping you see which one has the stronger performance on paper.

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: For European buyers, emissions details matter for running costs and where you can drive under local rules. Keeping these figures in the same label also makes cross-border paperwork feel a lot more manageable.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: Shows the Euro standard when listed. Helps you understand the vehicle’s compliance category at a glance.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)
subItems.description: Lists the CO₂ figure shown on the sticker, which can affect comparisons between similar cars and sometimes influences tax/fee calculations depending on the country.

subItems.title: Practical cross-border checks
subItems.description: If you’re importing a Ford into another country in Europe, the emissions info in one place helps you compare the seller’s claims with what your registration office requires, without having to sift through multiple documents.

categories.title: Basic Equipment
categories.description: This shows the core items you’d normally expect on that Ford trim level, helping you understand the baseline before options. Perfect when you’re scanning multiple listings and want quick clarity.

subItems.title: Everyday essentials
subItems.description: The regular daily-driver features most people rely on. It matters because it makes inspections faster and helps you confirm what should be there before you even show up.

subItems.title: Key safety essentials
subItems.description: The window sticker highlights safety-related essentials when listed. If a safety kit is important to you, this helps you check the right boxes without guessing.

meta.title: Toyota Window Sticker by VIN Lookup - Get Factory Options
meta.description: Get your Toyota Window Sticker by VIN & see factory specs, options, pricing & emissions. The most accurate & trusted window sticker lookup tool

hero.heading: See Toyota’s Factory Build with the Window Sticker by VIN
hero.description: Check a Toyota’s original factory equipment, installed options, and key vehicle details using the VIN. A clear way to understand how the car was built before you buy, sell, or compare.

whatIs.heading: What is a Toyota Window Sticker?
paragraphs: A Toyota Window Sticker shows all the essential info your Toyota had when it left the factory. Think of it as the car’s official record, listing trims, engine type, transmission, standard and optional equipment, original manufacturer’s suggested retail price (MSRP), and even Euro-compliant emissions.

It’s perfect for European Toyota enthusiasts who want clarity when buying or selling, or for collectors tracking every detail. And yes, you can get it straight from the VIN, even with older Toyota models with shorter VINs.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a Toyota Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: A Toyota Window Sticker by VIN is a full breakdown of the vehicle, from its identity to technical and safety details. You’ll find info on the model year, trim, VIN, engine, fuel system, emissions, MSRP, optional packages, standard equipment, and performance specs.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: This is your Toyota’s ID card. It lists the year, make, model, trim level, body style, and VIN. Perfect for double-checking that what you see matches what came from the factory.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: This is the quick “headline” for the car, the kind of line you want to match against the listing, the logbook, and your own eyes. For Toyota fans, it’s also where generations and facelifts quietly matter.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: Trim is where value lives. This part helps you understand what the Toyota was meant to be from the start, not what it was turned into later.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) is the vehicle’s unique fingerprint for decoding and matching records. It’s what makes a window sticker with VIN possible in the first place.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: This section covers features that come with the base Toyota model, including safety basics, comfort, and convenience. Great for comparing trims and seeing what was factory-installed.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: This is your daily-living list: things like climate features, infotainment basics, driver convenience items, and interior practicality. It’s also where you catch those subtle differences between model years.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: Safety equipment is a big reason people stick with Toyota in the first place. This part typically summarises the baseline safety set-up. The stuff the car was designed to have as standard. It’s useful for families, commuters, and anyone buying used who wants fewer question marks.

categories.title: Optional Equipment
categories.description: This section helps you separate factory-installed extras from later add-ons. It’s also the part collectors love, because original option combinations can affect desirability.

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: This part groups options the way the factory sold them, which is helpful when you’re verifying a trim claim

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: These options are standalone factory enhancements, represented by single selection items. They may range from minor convenience features to substantial technological upgrades, which can influence both the overall cost and daily user experience.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: Sometimes a feature isn’t an option at all; it’s simply included because the trim level is higher. This subsection helps you understand what the trim should include, so you don’t overpay for something that was standard anyway.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: See your Toyota’s original price. Base vehicle MSRP plus optional packages and destination fees, giving a full view of what the car costs brand new.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This is the starting point, the base price for that Toyota version before the extras. It’s useful as a reality check when someone claims, “This was insanely expensive new.”

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: Here’s where the extras add up. Options can push a Toyota into a different price bracket, and that can matter for resale logic. This subsection helps explain why two models of the same car don’t have the same value.

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: This includes all the technical bits, including the engine type, transmission, drivetrain layout, horsepower, torque, and more. Perfect for mechanics, enthusiasts, or buyers verifying specifications.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: This section summarises what’s powering the Toyota: engine family details and key specs. It’s especially handy when different engines exist under the same model name across Europe.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: Whether it’s automatic, manual, hybrid, or e-CVT style systems, the transmission shapes how the car drives and what it costs to live with. This part helps you verify what the vehicle should have, especially when adverts are vague.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: This section tells you if the Toyota has a front-wheel drive, all-wheel drive, and the layout differences that affect traction and running costs. For people in mountain regions or rainy coastal areas, the drivetrain isn’t a small detail. This is where you confirm it.

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: In this section, you will see the horsepower, torque, acceleration, and top speed. This helps you compare models and understand your Toyota’s real capabilities.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: Power figures help you compare variants fairly, especially across different European markets where naming conventions can confuse things. Seeing hp and kW makes it easier to match expectations with reality.

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: Torque and Performance figures provide key data about the engine’s capabilities, acceleration, and towing strength. This part helps you understand how the Toyota will behave beyond the brochure talk.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed
subItems.description: The acceleration and top speed matter for motorway driving confidence. This subsection offers quick reference performance figures that help you compare trims/engines without guessing

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: This block includes the Euro-standard emissions, CO₂ output, and practical cross-border compliance for Europe. Essential for anyone tracking environmental regulations or tax implications.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: This is where you’ll typically see the relevant Euro emissions classification context for the vehicle’s type-approval era. It helps when you’re checking compliance expectations in different cities or countries.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)
subItems.description: CO₂ g/km is one of the most-referenced numbers in European car shopping because it’s easy to compare and is often used in policy and consumer information displays. If you’re doing cross-border buying, having the CO₂ figure clearly stated can save time when you’re estimating costs.

subItems.title: Practical cross-border checks
subItems.description: If you’re buying a Toyota from another European country (or you’ve moved countries with your car), paperwork tends to multiply. With a Toyota window sticker, you can verify the story before you start paying fees and booking appointments.

categories.title: Basic Equipment
categories.description: Daily essentials and safety gear. This is the foundation of what makes a Toyota reliable and safe.

subItems.title: Everyday essentials
subItems.description: You will see the everyday essentials list, like seats, basic controls, and practical cabin features. The things you notice in the first 10 minutes of ownership.

subItems.title: Key safety essentials
subItems.description: A straight-to-the-point safety essentials recap. Helpful for quick comparisons, and good for peace of mind when you’re buying used for someone else.

meta.title: Subaru Window Sticker by VIN: Get Original Factory Specs
meta.description: Get the most accurate Subaru Window Sticker by VIN. See original specs, options, pricing, & emissions. Trusted, fast & built for Subaru in Europe

hero.heading: Get Your Subaru’s Original Factory Window Sticker
hero.description: Before buying a used Subaru, check how it originally left the factory. View equipment, options, and vehicle specifications linked to the VIN, all in one clear label.

whatIs.heading: What is a Subaru Window Sticker?
paragraphs: A Subaru Window Sticker is a factory-style summary of how a specific Subaru left production. It’s not a brochure, and it’s not a seller’s description. It lists the vehicle identity (year, model, VIN), the trim and body style, standard equipment, factory options, and key technical blocks like engine and emissions figures.

In Europe, where CO₂ (g/km) and fuel/energy consumption details can affect taxes, registration, and cross-border sales, having the original configuration in one place is simply practical. EU rules also push clear consumer information on fuel economy and CO₂ for new passenger cars, so these numbers matter.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a Subaru Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: A Subaru window sticker is a factory-based summary of how the vehicle was built when new. In Vehicle History Europe, you will see the Emissions Block that pulls in the CO₂ and related compliance context that often affects running costs and paperwork.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: This is the part you check before you trust anything else. If you’re from abroad, this block helps you avoid mix-ups, such as wrong model year, wrong trim, or even a copied listing that doesn’t match the car in front of you.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: Here, the sticker states the Subaru’s year and model clearly (for example, Forester or Outback may sound obvious, yet listings could get it wrong). It’s also useful when a car has been registered later than it was built.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: Subaru trims can look similar at a glance, especially after a few owners and a few cosmetic changes. This line anchors the exact trim and the body style as built.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: The VIN is the vehicle’s fingerprint, and the sticker ties everything back to it. In the window sticker, you will see the VIN on top of the label, helping ensure the vehicle’s identity, assembly location, and documented specifications properly match the physical automobile being evaluated.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: Standard equipment is what Subaru included on that trim before any paid extras. This matters because sellers often blend standard features and later modifications into one blurry list.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: This block lists the everyday items that define the cabin and its daily use. Such as seating and interior equipment, climate-related features, infotainment basics, and convenience hardware.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: This is the baseline safety set that came with the trim at build time. It helps you check whether a claimed safety package is truly factory-fitted or added later.

categories.title: Optional Equipment
categories.description: Options are where a Subaru becomes this Subaru. This section shows what was paid for at the factory, not what was bolted on later.

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: Packages bundle features together and can explain price jumps between trims that look identical in photos. If you’re hunting a specific setup, such as winter-focused packs, tech packs, or upgraded driver aids, this line tells you whether it was ordered that way.

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: These are standalone extras that weren’t tied to a full package. You’ll often see items here that owners brag about later, like upgraded wheels or specific comfort additions.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: Sometimes a feature isn’t an “option” as such. It’s included because of the trim. This subsection keeps that tidy, so you can see what the trim itself added versus what was separately selected.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: The Manufacturer’s Suggested Retail Price, or MSRP, serves as the factory’s recommended starting price (original factory pricing). It provides a clear foundation for pricing and helps consumers understand the value of their investment in a Subaru.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This is the starting figure for that Subaru trim when new. It helps you understand where the model sat in the range at the time of entry, mid, or top.

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: This line shows what the factory extras added to the price. It’s one of the easiest ways to see whether a car is truly optioned well.

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: Engine and drivetrain details affect maintenance planning, parts, towing suitability, and long-term ownership costs. If you’re comparing across EU markets, this block also helps align specs between similar names and trims.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: This summarises the engine family and key details that define how the Subaru drives and how it’s serviced. It’s also helpful when the same model name has multiple engine choices in different years.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: Transmission type and specification matter for reliability expectations and driving feel. This line confirms what was factory-fitted, which is useful when a car has had repairs or swaps.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: Subaru is famous for its drivetrain identity, but not every market and model year is identical in setup. This line confirms the drivetrain layout as built.

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: Power and torque figures, plus basic performance numbers, help you compare trims without getting misled by marketing names. If you’re buying across borders, it also helps align the vehicle with local insurance or registration categories where applicable.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: Power is commonly shown in hp and/or kW, and it’s useful for quick comparisons across EU listings. A window sticker lookup by VIN gives the factory figure tied to that exact configuration, not a forum estimate.

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: This block helps you compare a Subaru trim meant for relaxed driving versus one aimed at a stronger mid-range response. It also keeps expectations realistic when shopping online.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed
subItems.description: These are the headline numbers people ask about, even if they don’t plan on chasing them. In practice, they help compare variants and spot odd listings.

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: For European ownership, this block is more than “nice to know.” CO₂ figures can influence taxes, benefit-in-kind calculations in some contexts, and cross-border paperwork checks. The EU also requires consumer-facing CO₂ and fuel economy information for new cars, which is why these numbers are treated seriously.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: This line indicates the emissions standard classification relevant to the vehicle’s approval era. It’s useful when you’re checking city access rules or planning travel across countries with different low-emission zone rules.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)
subItems.description: CO₂ is often the number that gets quoted first in Europe because it can tie into costs and classification. Our VIN-based window sticker label keeps this figure paired to the exact engine and drivetrain combination.

subItems.title: Practical cross-border checks
subItems.description: If you’re importing within Europe, this is the block that helps you spot mismatches early and avoid paperwork surprises.

categories.title: Basic Equipment
categories.description: This is the quick-reference section for the everyday kit. If you’ve ever bought a car thinking it had a feature that turned out to be “optional on that trim,” you already know why this matters.

subItems.title: Everyday essentials
subItems.description: This covers the practical baseline, which is the items that make the car usable and comfortable in ordinary driving. It’s also handy for comparing a Subaru to another EU-market vehicle without getting lost in trim-name marketing.

subItems.title: Key safety essentials
subItems.description: A tidy list of the safety essentials included at the factory level. For buyers who are helping family members choose a car, this is an easy way to confirm the basics are there.

hero.heading: Check Dodge’s Original Build with a Window Sticker by VIN
hero.description: Buying a Dodge in Europe can feel like guesswork. A Dodge window sticker lookup by VIN tool helps you confirm the trim, packages, and original pricing before you pay, ship, or argue with a seller. Enter your VIN now to generate your Dodge window sticker.

whatIs.heading: What is a Dodge Window Sticker?
paragraphs: If you’re in Europe, owning a Dodge here can be a little complex. Different paperwork, different naming, and sometimes the seller’s story changes every time you ask a question.

That’s why our Dodge Window Sticker by VIN tool exists. You can just enter the VIN, and we generate a window sticker for the Dodge’s build receipt from the factory. Including the specifications, equipment, emissions block, and even MSRP, so you can stop guessing and start comparing.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a Dodge Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: A Dodge can look simple from the outside, but the value is in the details: trim naming, factory option packs, and those little line items. Our sticker layout is built for clarity, and it’s especially useful in Europe, where documents and listings don’t always match the original factory configuration.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: This top section is the identity card. It’s where a Dodge becomes a specific factory build with a traceable VIN. For buyers, it’s how you catch mismatched trims. For sellers, it’s how you prove the spec without writing a novel in the listing.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: This pinpoints the model year and the exact Dodge model name. It’s also helpful when European listings use different naming habits, or when a car is registered later than its build year.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: Trim and body style are crucial as they influence equipment, safety, value, and insurance classification. This part helps verify your Dodge’s actual trim.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: The window sticker includes the VIN, a unique character code (letters and numbers) that serves as a Dodge fingerprint and ties equipment and specs to one vehicle, not the whole model range.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: This is the factory baseline, the stuff a Dodge left the line with before anyone added dealer accessories or aftermarket parts. If you’re comparing two “same model” cars, standard equipment helps you understand what should be there on both, and what’s genuinely different.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: Covers everyday features (infotainment basics, seat functions, parking aids where applicable, the things you notice five minutes into a drive.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: Lists the core safety equipment fitted as standard. The factory equipment list you can quickly sanity-check.

categories.title: Optional Equipment

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: Bundle-style packages (e.g., appearance packs, tech packs, comfort packs). Great for spotting whether a claimed package is truly factory.

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: Some options aren’t packaged; they’re single choices. This section shows those standalone add-ons that are useful when a vehicle has one rare feature that collectors actually pay for.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: Some trims quietly include equipment upgrades that aren’t “options” in the normal sense. This section helps you see what came with the trim level itself.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: MSRP is a historical reference point showing how the Dodge was priced when new. It does not reflect today’s market value, taxes, or dealer markups, but it helps explain how the vehicle was positioned at launch.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This is the starting price for that Dodge configuration before extras. It gives you a clean baseline for value conversations and helps you understand whether a vehicle began life as an entry model or something more premium from day one.

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: Itemised pricing add-ons that show where the sticker price climbed, often the difference between “nice spec” and “full-fat.”

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: This section is for the people who care what’s under the bonnet. It’s the mechanical fingerprint: engine layout, capacity, and related drivetrain details in a quick-read format.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: This summarises the engine setup associated with that VIN. A key identifiers that help you confirm what’s actually under the bonnet.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: Shows the transmission type as tied to that VIN. This is important when listings casually say “auto” and leave it at that.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: Helps confirm whether it’s FWD/RWD/AWD (or equivalent), which matters a lot in real driving and resale.

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: Not everyone buys a Dodge for calm practicality. This section puts the numbers in one place so you can compare builds cleanly, especially across countries where listings mix kW/hp casually.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: Displays rated power figures in the common formats people expect in Europe. This is a clean reference point when you’re checking if a vehicle is correctly listed, or when a country-specific registration form wants power figures in a standard unit.

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: Torque is what you feel, not just what you brag about. This section summarises the factory-rated figures so you can compare trims and engines properly.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: Europe cares about emissions data in a very practical way. The EU requires consumer information on fuel economy and CO₂ emissions for new passenger cars at the point of sale, with standardised label requirements. That’s why this section is important.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: This indicates the relevant Euro emissions context. It’s useful when you’re checking whether a Dodge is likely to face restrictions in certain cities or whether it fits local registration requirements.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)

categories.title: Basic Equipment

hero.heading: Check the Original Window Sticker for Porsche Factory Build Details​
hero.description: Some Porsches may have missing features, be modified, or not match their original specs. A Porsche window sticker shows the MSRP, factory options, and full details to make sure you know what you’re buying.

whatIs.heading: What is a Porsche Window Sticker?
paragraphs: A Porsche Window Sticker is a detailed document that reveals the exact specifications and features of your Porsche when it was originally built. In Europe, this label acts as a snapshot of your car’s history, offering valuable insights into the trim, equipment, and even its recommended retail price from the manufacturer.

Whether you’re looking to verify your Porsche’s history or simply curious about its original specs, this document can be a real treasure. It helps identify everything from trim levels to standard and optional equipment.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a Porsche Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: Our Porsche Window Sticker reveals essential details about your vehicle’s factory configuration, giving you a complete breakdown of features, performance specs, and price at the time of purchase when you’re entering the VIN number.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: The Porsche Window Sticker starts with a brief description of the vehicle, detailing its model, trim, and unique features based on the VIN. This section gives a clear idea of what their car was like when it left the factory, tailored to the specific specifications of their vehicle.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: This section offers a snapshot of the car’s original year, make, and model. Helping you quickly identify what version of Porsche you’re dealing with, from the timeless 911 to the more recent Macan or Cayenne models.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: The trim level defines the model’s interior features, exterior design, and additional customizations. Whether it’s a base model or a sportier trim like the Turbo, this section tells you exactly what the factory installed when it came off the production line.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: Your VIN is the key to everything in the Porsche Window Sticker. In the label, you will see the VIN that shows all the details about your vehicle’s build, including where it was made, what options it came with, and even its place in Porsche’s production history.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: This section outlines the features that came standard with your Porsche, such as the base engine, interior, and safety features. It’s a great way to see what came with your car without having to look back at old brochures.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: The labels also show the details of the core features, like climate control, seating materials, and advanced tech systems, which are included here. It gives you a closer look at how your Porsche was designed to offer comfort on every drive.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: Porsche cars are built with safety in mind. From airbags to the safety features in the chassis, this section breaks down what the factory included to ensure your safety on the road.

categories.title: Optional Equipment
categories.description: Not everything comes standard on a Porsche. Some Porsches come with factory-optional equipment that adds value and customization to your vehicle; this section lists the features.

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: Factory option packages like sport seats, premium sound systems, or advanced driver-assist features might be listed here. These are features Porsche owners opted for to enhance their driving experience.

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: This area highlights any add-ons, such as upgraded wheels or custom paint finishes, that the original owner selected to make the car uniquely theirs.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: Trim-level additions include anything extra that’s specific to a model’s configuration, such as a different suspension setup or exclusive performance features found only on higher trims.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: The MSRP section shows the original list price set by the manufacturer when the Porsche was new. It reflects base pricing plus factory options, but it does not represent today’s resale or market value.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This is the starting price for your Porsche, without any add-ons or optional equipment. It gives you a good sense of what the base model cost when it was new.

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: This section outlines the costs of additional features, such as the premium package or custom wheels, helping to understand how much the owner invested in their Porsche.

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: The mechanical specs and engine details are crucial for any Porsche enthusiast. From horsepower to drivetrain layout, this section is where all the performance specs are stored.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: This part reveals what’s under the hood, like the engine size, type, and power output. For performance-oriented models, this section highlights the turbocharged engines, hybrid setups, or the famed flat-six engines Porsche is known for.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: Whether the Porsche has a manual, dual-clutch, or automatic transmission, this section on the window sticker will tell you how the car shifts and its overall driving experience.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: From rear-wheel drive to all-wheel drive, the drivetrain is an important element in your car’s handling. This section explains how power is delivered to the wheels.

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: Porsche’s performance is legendary, and this section explains how much power your car has, as well as its key performance figures like acceleration and top speed.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: This part reveals how much horsepower your Porsche engine produces. If the speed is the reason you buy a vehicle, this is an important number you need to look up.

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: Torque plays a big part in how fast a Porsche can accelerate, and this section highlights the torque figures and performance characteristics of your car.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed
subItems.description: Every Porsche has its claim to fame when it comes to speed. This section shows how fast your car can go, both in terms of acceleration and top-end speed.

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: The emissions block shows how your Porsche complies with European environmental standards. It includes official emissions data measured under the Worldwide Harmonized Light-Duty Vehicles Test Procedure (WLTP) system, which has been used across Europe since 2018.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: These figures matter because CO₂ values are often used for registration, taxation, and running-cost calculations in many European countries. When buying, selling, or importing a Porsche, accurate emissions data can help avoid delays and unexpected costs.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)
subItems.description: This section details the CO₂ emissions your Porsche produces, an important number for those who care about the environment or drive across European borders.

subItems.title: Practical cross-border checks
subItems.description: Porsche cars are built for global travel. The window sticker highlights what you need to know when crossing borders with your vehicle, from emissions to specific European requirements.

categories.title: Basic Equipment
categories.description: These parts of the window sticker list the basics in the car, like climate control, audio systems, and wheels, which are covered in this section. They’re essential, everyday features that make driving your Porsche a joy.

subItems.title: Everyday essentials
subItems.description: From the essential climate control to the interior features, this section breaks down what’s included to keep you comfortable and safe every time you drive.

subItems.title: Key safety essentials
subItems.description: Basic safety features are critical, and this section explains the minimum safety gear that comes standard in your Porsche, from airbags to stability control.

whatIs.heading: What is a BMW Window Sticker?
paragraphs: A BMW Window Sticker by VIN is a clean, factory-style overview of the BMW’s specification, built from the vehicle’s identification details. This production label shows “as-built” spec at the time it was first supplied: model and trim, standard equipment, factory options, key mechanical details, and the emissions information buyers often ask about in Europe.

It’s not a random decal or car sticker design for the windscreen. It’s a practical document for checking what’s original, what’s been added later, and what a seller might have missed. With Vehicle History Europe, you can generate a BMW Window Sticker by VIN for modern BMWs and many classic models that you can save and share.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a BMW Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: A BMW window sticker presents important information in a clear and organized manner, making it easy for buyers to compare vehicles. In Europe, buyers focus on CO₂ emissions and fuel economy due to tax implications and regulatory standards, making this information crucial for making informed purchasing decisions.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: This is the quick identity block. It’s the part you show someone first when they ask, “Which BMW is it, exactly?” It summarises the core build details in a way that is easy to match to listings, registration papers, and what’s sitting on your driveway.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: The year, BMW model line, and the specific variant are listed here. It sounds basic, but it prevents common mix-ups, especially with similar badges across generations. Helpful for buyers comparing two cars that “look the same” but are not the same.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: This section clarifies the trim and the body type (e.g., saloon, touring, coupé). On BMWs, trim wording can change by market and year, so having it presented plainly makes the rest of the sticker easier to interpret.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: The VIN is the anchor for the whole sticker. It’s the identifier used across Europe to connect the vehicle to its recorded spec and administrative documents. If you are moving a car between Member States, consistent identification is not optional; it’s the basis of the paperwork trail.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: This part shows what the BMW typically left the factory with, before any paid extras. It helps you spot what should be there, and what might be missing. It can also be good for maintenance planning because it hints at what systems the car was built to support.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: This covers the everyday items people actually notice: interior comfort, key cabin functions, and the “normal use” equipment that shapes how the BMW feels on a daily run. It’s also handy when comparing a base build with a higher trim that looks similar in photos.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: Here you’ll see the baseline safety equipment expected for that build. When you’re buying a used BMW, this is a quiet reality check, because it helps you understand what the car was designed with originally, before any later retrofits or aftermarket additions.

categories.title: Optional Equipment
categories.description: This section lists the paid options and packages that made the original order unique. If you’ve ever seen a seller claim “every option” on a BMW, this is where that statement either holds up or falls apart.

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: BMW option packages bundle several features together. This sub-section summarises those bundles so you can see what the factory grouped as a package, not what someone added later as a “nice extra”.

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: These are the single options chosen à la carte. Great for verifying the details that affect value, such as upgraded audio, special lighting, driver-assistance features, or interior upgrades that a listing might describe vaguely.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: Sometimes the trim itself carries additions beyond “standard”. This part notes those trim-linked extras so you can tell whether you’re looking at a genuine trim build or a badge-and-wheels situation.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: This section shows the recommended retail price presented as a structured reference point for how the car was positioned when new. It’s not meant to predict today’s used value. Instead, it helps owners and buyers see what was “base” and what was paid extra at the time.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This is the starting price for the BMW in that configuration, before options. It gives a clean baseline when you are comparing two similar cars where one has far more equipment than the other.

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: This summarises the cost impact of options and packages as listed on the sticker. It’s useful when you are trying to understand why one example commands a premium.

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: This block is for the practical questions. What engine is it, what transmission is it paired with, and how is power delivered? For cross-border buyers, it can also help when you are trying to match a vehicle to the right category for servicing and parts.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: Engine displacement and key identifiers sit here, presented clearly enough for non-specialists. It helps you avoid the common “same badge, different engine” surprise that pops up in multi-market European listings.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: The label also shows the details about the automatic or manual transmission type. It’s a small line with big consequences for value, driving feel, and sometimes even insurance quotes.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: The stickers list whether the BMWs have front, rear, or all-wheel drive. With BMW, drivetrain details can be central to the buying decision, especially if you are choosing between a daily commuter setup and a more enthusiast-focused layout.

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: This part translates the mechanical spec into numbers people recognise when comparing BMWs. It’s not “bragging rights”. It’s a quick way to confirm you are looking at the correct output for that build.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: Power is shown in common units, so it’s easy to compare across adverts and countries. It helps when a listing only shows one unit, or when two similar cars have different outputs due to engine variants.

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: Torque and related performance notes are listed to support real-world comparisons, like motorway driving and overtakes. It also helps explain why two BMWs with similar horsepower can feel different on the road.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed
subItems.description: The sticker also listed the acceleration and top speed when the BMW came from the manufacturer. Buyers use it as a sense-check, not as a promise. If a claimed figure looks off, it can signal a mismatched model or incorrect listing.

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: This section matters if you’re European or want to buy or sell the BMW in the Europe market. It is often tied to costs and access: taxation bands, registration fees, and restrictions in certain urban areas. The label keeps these details in one place for quick checks.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: This states the Euro emissions standard associated with the build, where available. In day-to-day terms, it helps owners understand how the car may be treated under local rules and city access policies.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)
subItems.description: CO₂ figures are included because consumer information on fuel economy and CO₂ for new passenger cars has long been treated as something buyers should be able to compare. It’s also one of the first numbers people ask for when importing or registering in another EU country.

subItems.title: Practical cross-border checks
subItems.description: This is the “use it in real life” sub-section: the parts people check when moving between countries or buying from abroad. For example, if you’re purchasing in Ireland and registering elsewhere, or importing into Ireland, having emissions and identity details clearly listed saves back-and-forth.

categories.title: Basic Equipment
categories.description: This section highlights the “daily essentials” for quick reading. Not everyone wants to sift through a lengthy equipment list, especially when comparing multiple BMWs.

subItems.title: Everyday essentials
subItems.description: This covers the practical items that shape daily use. It’s also where you sanity-check claims like “fully loaded” or “high spec” against what is actually present.

subItems.title: Key safety essentials
subItems.description: A straightforward list of safety essentials, presented for quick comparison. It’s handy when you are checking two similar cars and want to see whether one has meaningful safety differences.

whatsOn.heading: What's on a Hyundai Window Sticker?
whatsOn.introParagraph: A Hyundai window sticker is packed with details that can tell you everything about the car, from its basic features to its most intricate options. For example, the sticker lists the car’s trim level, body style, engine type, and any special features it came with straight from the factory.

categories.title: Vehicle Description
categories.description: The vehicle description section on a Hyundai window sticker gives you a quick overview of the car’s make, model, and year. It’s like a snapshot of the car when it first hit the showroom floor. For those of you who are enthusiasts or collectors, this section brings back memories of how the car was first marketed and sold.

subItems.title: Vehicle year, make, and model
subItems.description: This section shows you the year the Hyundai was made, the specific make, and the model it belongs to. Whether you have a classic or a modern Hyundai, this part of the sticker helps you connect the car to the time it was built and provides important historical context.

subItems.title: Trim level and body style
subItems.description: The Hyundai’s trim level and body style give you insights into the specific configuration of the vehicle. Whether you have a sleek sedan or a rugged SUV, this part of the sticker breaks down the key features that make the car stand out from others.

subItems.title: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)
subItems.description: The VIN is a unique code that identifies the Hyundai, ensuring it’s the same car that was originally sold. This number is important for verifying the car’s build data.

categories.title: Standard Equipment
categories.description: The standard equipment section lists the key features that came with it, straight from the factory. This could include anything from air conditioning to power windows. It’s a helpful tool for understanding exactly what the vehicle had when it was first built.

subItems.title: Core comfort and convenience features
subItems.description: This part of the label highlights the comfort and convenience features that come standard in the Hyundai vehicles, such as seating options, climate control, and more. If you’re someone who values those little luxuries, this section will give you a solid idea of what the vehicle offered.

subItems.title: Safety basics
subItems.description: Safety features are essential, and this section covers the basic safety options your Hyundai was equipped with. From airbags to traction control, it outlines the standard features that help keep you safe on the road.

categories.title: Optional Equipment
categories.description: In addition to standard equipment, many Hyundai vehicles come with optional features that can be added to personalize your ride. This section of the window sticker details all the options that could have been added at the factory.

subItems.title: Factory option packages
subItems.description: Factory option packages are bundles of features designed to enhance the car. Whether you opted for a sports package or added premium audio, this part of the sticker will tell you which packages your car was equipped with.

subItems.title: Individual add-on options
subItems.description: Individual add-ons are special features you may have chosen when purchasing the car. This could include a sunroof, upgraded wheels, or even advanced tech options. It’s all about making your Hyundai truly yours.

subItems.title: Trim-level additions
subItems.description: If the car has unique trim-level options, this section will explain the specific additions that set your car apart. Whether it’s a sportier version or a luxury upgrade, these additions make a big difference.

categories.title: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
categories.description: The MSRP section is where you’ll find the original price of the car. It’s a way to see just how much your car was worth when it was new, including both the base price and any optional package or add-ons.

subItems.title: Base vehicle MSRP
subItems.description: This shows the base price of the Hyundai before any optional add-ons or upgrades. It’s an essential reference point for understanding the starting price of your car.

subItems.title: Factory options and packages cost
subItems.description: If you added any options or packages to the Hyundai when purchasing, this section will break down their costs. From upgraded features to premium finishes, it shows you exactly what you paid for.

categories.title: Mechanical and Engine Information
categories.description: The mechanical and engine section dives into the technical side of the car. From engine specs to transmission details, this section tells you what’s under the hood and how your car performs.

subItems.title: Engine specifications
subItems.description: Here, you’ll find the engine specifications, such as the engine size, type, and horsepower. This information gives you a deeper understanding of Hyundai’s performance.

subItems.title: Transmission
subItems.description: The transmission section highlights the type of transmission the Hyundai has, whether it’s manual or automatic. It’s an important detail for understanding how your car shifts and performs.

subItems.title: Drivetrain layout
subItems.description: This shows the drivetrain layout of the Hyundai, whether it’s front-wheel drive, rear-wheel drive, or all-wheel drive. It helps you understand how your car handles in different conditions.

categories.title: Factory Power and Performance
categories.description: The power and performance section gives you an in-depth look at how the car performs. It includes details about horsepower, torque, and acceleration.

subItems.title: Power output (hp / kW)
subItems.description: The power output section tells you how much horsepower (or kW) the car has. It’s all about understanding the engine’s performance and what you can expect from the car.

subItems.title: Torque and performance figures
subItems.description: Torque is another key performance metric. This section tells you how much torque the car delivers and how it impacts the car’s acceleration and handling.

subItems.title: Acceleration and top speed
subItems.description: Here, you’ll get the performance figures for acceleration and top speed, which can tell you how fast the Hyundai can go and how quickly it can reach those speeds.

categories.title: Emissions Block
categories.description: The emissions section provides important information about how the car meets environmental standards, including CO₂ emissions and the Euro emissions standard.

subItems.title: Euro emissions standard
subItems.description: The applicable Euro emissions classification is listed clearly, helping buyers understand regulatory compatibility.

subItems.title: CO₂ emissions (g/km)
subItems.description: This section provides details about the official CO₂ figures are shown as recorded by the manufacturer, useful for tax and compliance checks.

subItems.title: Practical cross-border checks
subItems.description: This part helps identify whether the Hyundai’s factory emissions data aligns with requirements in other European countries, reducing surprises during registration.

categories.title: Basic Equipment
categories.description: Basic equipment covers essential features present regardless of trim or options, forming the baseline specification.

subItems.title: Everyday essentials
subItems.description: Items necessary for daily driving, comfort, and usability appear here, giving a grounded view of what was included as standard.

subItems.title: Key safety essentials
